reasoning: The reference class for this problem is the daily random walk of the 30-year fixed FHA mortgage rate, which tends to follow the 10-year Treasury yield. Given that the recent trajectory of both the OBMMI FHA 30-year rate and the 10-year Treasury has been slightly downward to flat (with rates gently sliding from around 6.05% in early January to 5.987% on January 16, and the 10-year Treasury drifting from ~4.17% to ~4.13%), the short-term trend is marginally negative. The FRED tool's linear trend model also predicts a slightly lower rate (5.918) by January 25, estimating a 28% chance of being above the most recent value, though seasonal effects bump the combined estimate closer to 47%. Because the rate environment heading into late January (ahead of the likely Jan 27-28 FOMC meeting) leans toward a minor continuation of this easing or stabilization, I assign a probability slightly below 50% for January 25 (44%). For February 17, which is further out and incorporates outcomes from the FOMC meeting and additional economic data, the variance increases, pulling the probability closer to the 50% baseline (47%).
